div1.entryFree:
Bathe, 1) trans. to immerse, to wash as in a bath: the crow may b. his coal-black wings in mire, Lucr. 1009. in Lucrece 'bleeding stream he falls and --s the pale fear in his face, Lucr. 1009 these often --d she in her fluxive eyes, Compl. 50. when tears our recountments had most kindly --d, As IV, 3, 141. --d thy growing with our heated bloods, H6C II, 2, 169. --d in maiden blood, Tit. II, 3, 232. b. their hands in it, Caes. II, 2, 79. b. our hands in Caesar's blood, III, 1, 106. b. my dying honour in the blood, Ant. IV, 2, 6. had I this cheek to b. my lips upon, Cymb. I, 6, 100.
2) intr., to be in a bath, to be immersed in a fluid as in a bath: she --s in water, yet her fire must burn, Ven. 94. to b. in fiery floods, Meas. III, 1, 122. eagles having lately --d, H4A IV, 1, 99. in which so many smiling Romans --d, Caes. II, 2, 86. to b. in reeking wounds, Mcb. I, 2, 39. chaste Dian --ing, Cymb. II, 4, 82.
